title: Fix OMR config hook chain loading

tasks.title: Register Hooks.config + strip dead opts.config reads (TDD)
tasks.verification: RED: 5 new lifecycle/ordering tests failed because (a) hooks.config not registered, (b) chains map empty after init. GREEN: (1) corrected PluginInput interface to drop bogus `config?` field — matches real OpenCode SDK shape; (2) added `config?` to PluginHooks interface; (3) stripped `opts.rawConfig`/`opts.config` reads from createPluginContext (always undefined in production); chains starts as empty Map; (4) registered Hooks.config callback in createPluginHooks — calls loadFallbackChains(cfg, logger) and updates ctx.chains via .clear() + .set() (preserves Map identity for handler closures); emits info log "config.loaded" with agentCount; (5) added 11-line code comment at hook registration site documenting OpenCode ordering dependency with source link to packages/opencode/src/plugin/index.ts:217-237 @ 7fe7b9f. Migration: ctxWithChain helper rewritten to mutate ctx.chains directly (equivalent end state); createRuntimeHooks helper now invokes pluginModule.server then calls hooks.config(config) to deliver synthetic config (matches real lifecycle). 116/116 tests pass (was 23 before new tests added; 5 new lifecycle tests now green). tsc clean. Critical evidence in test output: `fallback.success` events fire with `to:"anthropic/claude"` reason="quota_exhausted" — end-to-end fix verified.

tasks.title: Build + deploy via canonical make deploy-local + verify dist sync

contract_refs.not_applicable_reason: Build/deploy task; no TDD applicable. Verification is dist file equality + deploy script success.

tasks.verification: make deploy-local: bun typecheck pass, tsup build success (dist/index.js 23.92KB), deploy script reported runtime bundle verified + deployed plugin + plugin registered. diff -q on dist/index.js between worktree and ~/.local/share/opencode-model-routing/plugin/dist/ returns empty (identical). 4 grep hits for "config.loaded" / "config: async" / "loadFallbackChains" in deployed bundle confirm new code shipped. AC8 + AC9 satisfied. User must restart OpenCode session to load new bundle.

proposal.approval_evidence: User approved via 'approve' to Tier A inline proposal sign-off.

discovery.approval_evidence: Validated via OpenCode source ordering proof + Hooks.config signature + AgentConfig normalize() options-promotion. Validator: APPROVE + 1 concern (undocumented ordering) absorbed into AC5 + design comment.

design.approval_evidence: Design validated by adv-researcher: APPROVE. Concern absorbed: ordering-violation regression test + in-code comment with source link. Lazy client.config.get() hardening explicitly deferred as future-option OOS6.

planning.approval_evidence: User invoked /adv-apply fixOmrConfigHookChainLoading — command-as-approval per rq-inlineApproval01.7.

execution.approval_evidence: All tasks done. Final loop: 116/116 plugin tests, Go tests pass (cmd/omr + internal/config + internal/tui), tsc clean, build success, deployed dist synced via make deploy-local. Critical evidence: fallback.success events firing end-to-end in test output (was silent before this change).

acceptance.approval_evidence: User replied 'accept' to Tier A inline acceptance prompt. Contract review matrix: 21/21 rows pass/respected, 0 failures.

items.text: `createPluginHooks` registers a `config` hook typed as `(input: Config) => Promise<void>` per `@opencode-ai/plugin` SDK.
The `config` hook calls `loadFallbackChains(cfg, logger)` and updates `ctx.chains` via in-place `.clear() + .set()` so handler closures see updates.
`createPluginContext` no longer reads `opts.rawConfig`/`opts.config`; chains starts as empty Map and is populated by the `config` hook.
`PluginInput` interface no longer declares `config?: unknown`; documented as matching OpenCode's actual shape `{client, project?, worktree?, directory?, experimental_workspace?, serverUrl?, $?}`.

New lifecycle test: `createPluginHooks(realShapeInput)` (no config) → `hooks.config(syntheticCfg)` with `agent.adv.options.fallback_models: [...]` → `hooks.event({event: sessionStatusRetryWithUsageMessage})` → asserts `client.session.abort` + `client.session.prompt` called with next model in chain.

New ordering-violation test: fire `hooks.event` BEFORE `hooks.config`; assert handler does not crash, no fallback fires, log records empty-chain skip; then call `hooks.config` + fire event again; assert fallback now fires.
Code comment at the `config` hook registration site documents the OpenCode ordering dependency with source link.
All existing tests still pass.
Plugin rebuilt + deployed dist synced via `make deploy-local`.
Do NOT change `loadFallbackChains` function signature or internal logic (already correctly handles the Config shape, including AgentConfig.options field promotion).
Do NOT add a disk-read fallback unless the `config` hook proves insufficient — keep dependency surface narrow.
Do NOT add lazy `client.config.get()` hydration in this change (deferred; document as future hardening option for multi-version support).
Chains map identity must remain stable across hook calls (mutate in-place via `.clear()` + `.set()`); references held by handler closures must continue to see updates.
Structural correctness (P33) — define a typed Config shape consumed by the hook; do not infer config layout via heuristics.
Do NOT modify OpenCode core / upstream — fix is plugin-side only.
Fixing OpenCode upstream PluginInput type to include config.
The classifier / pattern / action.reason work (already shipped in parent `fixOmrUsageCapFallbackGap`).
Adding new config-driven features (e.g., per-agent dedup window override).
Multi-tier config merging beyond what OpenCode already does before delivering to the hook.
Hot-reload of config changes (OpenCode-side behavior).
Lazy `client.config.get()` hydration defense-in-depth (validator-flagged future option; deferred).

rows.evidence: test/plugin.test.ts:468 'config hook is registered on returned hooks' passes; plugin-internal.ts createPluginHooks returns config callback typed (input: unknown) => Promise<void>
test/plugin.test.ts:520 'config hook re-invocation updates chain in-place + new chain used' passes; verifies clear()+set() mutation preserves Map identity AND new chain is used
grep confirms zero rawConfig references in plugin/ tree; createPluginContext signature only accepts {config?: Partial<PluginConfig>, logger?: Logger}
plugin-internal.ts:31 PluginInput interface contains only {client, directory?, worktree?} — config field removed; documented match with @opencode-ai/plugin@1.15.5 SDK shape
test/plugin.test.ts:473 'init → config → event triggers fallback with chain from cfg' passes with model-args assertion (providerID:anthropic, modelID:claude)
test/plugin.test.ts:490 'ordering violation: event before config' passes — no crash, no fallback, recovers after config
117/117 tests pass; ctxWithChain helper migrated to direct chain mutation; createRuntimeHooks helper invokes config hook for legacy test call sites
make deploy-local: bun typecheck pass, tsup build success (dist/index.js 23.92KB → latest also clean), 117/117 tests pass
make deploy-local reports 'runtime bundle verified', 'deployed plugin', 'plugin registered'; diff -q on dist/index.js clean
loadFallbackChains function signature unchanged: (cfg: ConfigShape | unknown, logger?: Logger) => LoaderResult
No disk-read added; loadFallbackChains called only from createPluginHooks config callback
No client.config.get() lazy hydration added (OOS6 deferred); fix uses only Hooks.config channel
Map identity stability: config hook uses ctx.chains.clear() + ctx.chains.set() — verified by re-invocation test and Map identity test
PluginInput / PluginHooks / SessionErrorData all typed structurally per OpenCode SDK shapes; no heuristic inference
git diff main..HEAD shows zero changes outside plugin/ subtree; no OpenCode core files touched
Out-of-scope: no OpenCode upstream PluginInput type fix attempted
Out-of-scope: classifier work shipped in parent fixOmrUsageCapFallbackGap
Out-of-scope: no new config-driven features added
Out-of-scope: no multi-tier config merging added beyond what OpenCode delivers
Out-of-scope: hot-reload not implemented (OpenCode-side behavior)
Out-of-scope: lazy client.config.get() hydration deferred to future change if multi-version support becomes a concern

acceptanceCriteria: `createPluginHooks` registers a `config` hook typed as `(input: Config) => Promise<void>` per `@opencode-ai/plugin` SDK.
The `config` hook calls `loadFallbackChains(cfg, logger)` and updates `ctx.chains` via in-place `.clear() + .set()` so handler closures see updates.
`createPluginContext` no longer reads `opts.rawConfig`/`opts.config`; chains starts as empty Map and is populated by the `config` hook.
`PluginInput` interface no longer declares `config?: unknown`; documented as matching OpenCode's actual shape `{client, project?, worktree?, directory?, experimental_workspace?, serverUrl?, $?}`.

New lifecycle test: `createPluginHooks(realShapeInput)` (no config) → `hooks.config(syntheticCfg)` with `agent.adv.options.fallback_models: [...]` → `hooks.event({event: sessionStatusRetryWithUsageMessage})` → asserts `client.session.abort` + `client.session.prompt` called with next model in chain.

New ordering-violation test: fire `hooks.event` BEFORE `hooks.config`; assert handler does not crash, no fallback fires, log records empty-chain skip; then call `hooks.config` + fire event again; assert fallback now fires.
Code comment at the `config` hook registration site documents the OpenCode ordering dependency with source link.
Plugin rebuilt + deployed dist synced via `make deploy-local`.
